absence_records.reason: A broad compare-the-options affordance would duplicate trade-offs, prioritization, expected-value, or decision-trees. Opportunity cost earns a separate Batch 3a record only when it identifies the real next-best use displaced by the current commitment.

candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

case_evidence_needed: The decision under approval and the scarce resource it consumes: people, budget, calendar time, launch window, leadership attention, trust, or optionality.
diagnostic_questions: What is the single best executable alternative use of the same people, budget, launch window, or attention?
do_not_use_when: The real alternatives, constraints, or objective function are not framed well enough for comparison without false precision.
misuse_guards: Do not count unrealistic alternatives that the team cannot actually fund, execute, or own.

source_evidence.source_quote: The fundamental essence of this model is the value of the best alternative opportunity you didn't choose

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name the best real alternative displaced by the current yes, not a vague list of possibilities or a decorative comparison set.
evidence_required: The current commitment, continuation, renewal, feature, market, or project under consideration.
good_output_shape: States the current yes and the next-best alternative in the same operational currency.

use_when: A roadmap addition, renewal, sprint, vendor, project, or channel looks cheap because the explicit spend is small while the same people, budget, launch window, or attention are needed elsewhere.

danger_when: The option set is still poorly framed, so comparison would create false precision around the wrong alternatives or objective function.

failure_modes.description: Commitment can hide what becomes impossible once resources, time, or attention are locked into one path.
failure_modes.mitigation: Before committing, explicitly list what becomes impossible: name the specific projects, options, or capabilities that this commitment forecloses.

heuristics.description: Upgrade simple pro-con thinking into a comparison that includes the implicit costs of the best foregone option.

premortem_questions.description: Before approval: name the single most valuable thing this decision makes impossible or significantly harder for the next 12 months.
premortem_questions.source_quote: Before approving: name the single most valuable thing this decision makes impossible or significantly harder for the next 12 months.

select_when: A team wants to add one more initiative even though the same people, budget, or launch window are needed by a higher-leverage move.

why_pulled.reason: The advice commits the same budget and launch window without naming the displaced alternative.

absence_records.reason: The source supports specific testability, disconfirming evidence, and reversal thresholds. Generic skepticism would not improve Decision Pressure dismissal or tripwire quality.

candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

case_evidence_needed: The live hypothesis, recommendation, assumption, or theory that could be wrong.
diagnostic_questions: What single fact, if true, would cause us to abandon this hypothesis immediately?
do_not_use_when: The work is still so vague that a hypothesis would be decorative rather than testable.
misuse_guards: Do not use falsifiability language without naming an actual disconfirming test, threshold, or observation.

source_evidence.source_quote: The fundamental essence of Falsifiability is the principle that **a theory or hypothesis must be capable of being proven false**.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to state the concrete observation, threshold, or fact that would cause the thesis or recommendation to be abandoned before commitment proceeds.
evidence_required: The thesis, claim, plan, or recommendation under review.
good_output_shape: Names the claim and the reversal condition in the same paragraph.

use_when: A recommendation, hypothesis, plan, or strategic thesis is strong enough to shape commitment but has not named what would prove it wrong.

danger_when: The work is still so vague or underscoped that any initial hypothesis would be decorative rather than testable.

failure_modes.description: The need to be right can overpower the search for truth and block disconfirming evidence.
failure_modes.mitigation: Define what evidence would disprove the hypothesis before seeking confirmation; the need to be right is the enemy of the search for truth.

heuristics.description: Use strong, testable hypotheses because they are easier to challenge and pressure test than vague claims.

premortem_questions.description: What single fact, if true, would cause us to abandon this hypothesis immediately?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What single fact, if true, would cause us to abandon this hypothesis immediately?

select_when: The team has a strong thesis and needs a disciplined way to prove it wrong before committing.

why_pulled.reason: The assistant treats the rollout thesis as plausible without naming what evidence would reverse it.

absence_records.reason: The source supports ranges, sensitivity, and humility about uncertainty. A point-estimate affordance would create the false precision the model warns against.

candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

case_evidence_needed: The decision, bet, forecast, or recommendation currently being framed as certain or binary.
diagnostic_questions: What probability range matters for this decision, and what would narrow that range?
do_not_use_when: Probability language is postponing a commitment after the key economics, downside, and action threshold are already clear.
misuse_guards: Do not present exact-looking numbers with more precision than the evidence supports.

source_evidence.source_quote: Probabilistic Thinking (PT) is a mental model centered on **estimating the parameters of uncertainty**.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to state an honest probability or confidence range only when the range will change the commitment, not as decorative precision.
evidence_required: The uncertain claim, forecast, or bet under consideration.
good_output_shape: Uses a range rather than a single exact-looking estimate when uncertainty is material.

use_when: The decision is more-likely-or-less-likely rather than yes-or-no, and binary framing would hide a meaningful uncertainty range.

danger_when: The work requires a concrete commitment now, but the team is using probability language to postpone choosing after the key economics are already clear.

failure_modes.description: Over-reliance on the mean can hide low-probability, high-impact outcomes that matter more than the base case.
failure_modes.mitigation: Report the full distribution, not just the expected value: require decision-makers to acknowledge tail scenarios and state what level of downside they can absorb.

heuristics.description: Frame decisions as expected-value calculations instead of looking for options with no downside at all.

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?

select_when: The decision is not yes-or-no but more-likely-or-less-likely, so binary framing would hide meaningful uncertainty.

why_pulled.evidence_quote: we probably just need to decide now
why_pulled.reason: The user frame collapses uncertain renewal and launch outcomes into a go/no-go decision.

absence_records.reason: The source supports a pause that improves the next move, not open-ended reflection. It explicitly warns that the pause can become a hiding place for avoiding commitment.

candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

case_evidence_needed: The immediate action, analysis, or detail work the team is immersed in.
diagnostic_questions: What governing structure or core point is being obscured by the current details?
do_not_use_when: Reflection is being used to postpone commitment, avoid trade-offs, or keep options abstract.
misuse_guards: Do not use step-back as a socially acceptable way to avoid commitment.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to step back from detail immersion and name the governing structure, core point, or problem-owner frame before recommending execution.
evidence_required: The detail work, analysis, or action currently absorbing attention.
good_output_shape: Names what matters more than the current detail stream.

use_when: The team is too close to the details to see what actually matters.

danger_when: The pause is becoming a hiding place for avoiding commitment, trade-offs, or concrete next steps.

failure_modes.description: Reflection can become a hiding place that postpones commitment, trade-offs, or the next concrete move.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a reflection deadline: agree in advance on when the pause must produce a concrete next action, trade-off, or commitment.
failure_modes.mode: Reflection can become a hiding place that postpones commitment, trade-offs, or the next concrete mov...
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When the Pause Becomes a Hiding Place: The model fails when reflection is used to postpone commitment, avoid trade-offs, or keep options abstract rather than to improve the next concrete move.

heuristics.description: Use the pause to recover the big picture and governing purpose before spending more effort on local detail.
heuristics.source_quote: The step back forces the articulation of the main goal or core message, ensuring every subsequent action is aligned with a single purpose.

select_when: Immediate immersion is obscuring the structure of the problem and a deliberate pause would likely improve the next move.

why_pulled.evidence_quote: we are running out of time
why_pulled.reason: The case is moving from urgency into commitment before restating the governing purpose.

absence_records.reason: The source does not support treating constraints as permanent rules. It warns that old constraints can become misleading when the environment changes.

candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

case_evidence_needed: The goal, scope, decision, or problem surface being constrained.
diagnostic_questions: What boundary, rule, or limitation defines the scope of this decision?
do_not_use_when: The constraint is inherited, outdated, or untested after the economics, technology, or stakeholder environment has changed.
misuse_guards: Do not treat old constraints as permanent without checking whether the environment changed.

source_evidence.source_quote: boundary, rule, or limitation that defines the functional scope, responsibilities, or acceptable parameters of a system, problem, or endeavor

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to state the scope boundary, success constraint, acceptable trade-off, and deliberate exclusion before recommending action.
evidence_required: The decision or workstream being bounded.
good_output_shape: Names the constraint in operational terms.

use_when: Too many goals, variables, stakeholders, or possible moves are competing for attention and the team is losing clarity.

danger_when: Old constraints are remaining unquestioned after the economics, technology, or stakeholder environment has changed.

failure_modes.description: Stale constraints can quietly mislead action when the environment changed but the boundary never did.
failure_modes.mitigation: Revisit constraints on a schedule: ask whether each boundary still reflects the current environment or has fossilized from a prior context.
failure_modes.mode: Danger when old constraints remain unquestioned after the economics, technology, or stakeholder environment has changed.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when old constraints remain unquestioned after the economics, technology, or stakeholder environment has changed.

heuristics.description: Define constraints explicitly in the problem statement so the scope is realistic before analysis starts.

premortem_questions.description: How does what you’re doing solve the problem?
premortem_questions.source_quote: How does what you’re doing solve the problem?

select_when: A team is losing clarity because too many goals, variables, or stakeholders are competing for attention at once.

why_pulled.reason: The plan does not state which scope, budget, or launch constraints govern the trade-off.

absence_records.reason: The source supports evidence-backed link verification, not sign-off accumulation. It explicitly warns that added checks can become verification theater if they do not improve the truth of the chain.

candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

case_evidence_needed: The conclusion or commitment under review and the linked premises it depends on.
diagnostic_questions: Which link in this reasoning chain would collapse the whole conclusion if it were false?
do_not_use_when: The situation needs a reversible probe or flexible engagement more than exhaustive step-by-step verification.
misuse_guards: Do not use chain-of-verification to justify endless sign-offs when a bounded reversible test would create better learning.

source_evidence.source_quote: evidence to back up each link in that chain

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name the conclusion, its linked premises, and the premise that would collapse the conclusion before expressing confidence.
evidence_required: The conclusion, plan, or hypothesis being advanced.
good_output_shape: Shows the chain of premises in order.

use_when: A recommendation, forecast, rollout, or diagnosis depends on several linked premises that must all hold for the conclusion to survive.

danger_when: Verification is becoming theater through extra checks and sign-offs that do not materially improve the truth of the chain.

failure_modes.description: A flawed foundation can survive upward because teams fail to challenge weak assumptions at the earliest link.
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge assumptions at the earliest link: a flawed foundation will survive all later checks if the first step is treated as given.

heuristics.description: Break the main claim into smaller conditions that can be validated bottom-up before recommitting to the top-line answer.
heuristics.source_quote: The verification happens bottom-up: analyses, facts, and data are gathered to prove or disprove the elementary hypotheses, and only once these are validated can the problem solver "climb back up the pyramid" to confirm the leading hypothesis.

premortem_questions.description: Which links are make-or-break enough that the whole conclusion collapses if they fail?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Priority is given to critical, "make or break" hypotheses that, if proven wrong, would collapse the entire hypothesis pyramid.

select_when: A case depends on several premises in sequence and one weak link could collapse the whole conclusion.

why_pulled.reason: The assistant defers verification until after action even though several premises must hold in sequence.

absence_records.reason: The source warns that teams can become good at naming confirmation bias in others while leaving their own evidence standards untouched. A useful affordance must change the evidence test, not assign blame.

candidate_cards.llm_instruction: Consider, merge, set aside, or ignore. Do not force use. Do not treat coverage labels as final semantic selection.

case_evidence_needed: The favored conclusion, sponsor-backed recommendation, or preferred thesis under review.
diagnostic_questions: What evidence would prove this recommendation wrong?
do_not_use_when: The answer would merely accuse others of bias without changing the evidence standard or decision test.
misuse_guards: Do not use confirmation-bias language to accuse other people while leaving your own evidence standard unchanged.

source_evidence.source_quote: tendency to continuously filter for and focus on incoming data points that confirm our current beliefs, attitudes, and opinions

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to bring disconfirming observations, objections, failed cases, and quiet losses into the same comparison set as visible confirming evidence.
evidence_required: The confirming evidence currently carrying the recommendation.
good_output_shape: Names the confirming evidence and the excluded counter-evidence side by side.

use_when: A team is converging quickly on a favored recommendation before the evidence is fully in.

danger_when: The review is already structured around explicit falsification, equal treatment of confirming and disconfirming evidence, and adversarial challenge.

failure_modes.description: Bias blind spot amplification lets the team audit everyone else's evidence standards while sparing its own.
failure_modes.mitigation: Turn the diagnostic lens inward first: audit your own evidence standards with the same rigor you apply to others.

heuristics.description: Use the Darwinian approach by trying to kill weak ideas before defending favored ones.

premortem_questions.description: What would we have to believe for this conclusion to be true, and which of those assumptions is weakest?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

select_when: A team is converging on a favored answer before the evidence has been fully tested.

why_pulled.reason: The transaction suggests convergence on the preferred renewal answer before disconfirming evidence is named.

review_notes: Review-only packet built from explicit nominations; no live lanes were run.
Graph-only cards are eligible recall material but not reviewed affordance depth.
LLM or reviewer owns semantic selection, merging, ignoring, and final wording.

why_pulled.reason: Duplicate nomination included to prove suppression is visible instead of silently erased.

transaction_context.assistant_advice_summary: The assistant recommends moving quickly, negotiating lightly, and tracking risks after launch, but it does not name the displaced alternative, kill criteria, or verification chain.
transaction_context.known_action_or_commitment: Renew the vendor and launch within two weeks unless a review changes the commitment.
transaction_context.user_situation_summary: A team is about to renew a vendor contract and rush a product rollout after receiving plausible AI advice that frames speed as the main win.
